problem_statement: Existing food ordering methods are often inefficient, lack real-time tracking, and offer limited menu browsing options, leading to user frustration and inaccurate orders.
goals: Enable users to easily browse menus from various restaurants.
Allow users to place food orders conveniently and securely.
Provide real-time tracking of food delivery status.
Improve the overall user experience of ordering food online.
user_personas: Hungry Customer: Wants to order food quickly, browse options, and track delivery.
Restaurant Owner/Manager: Needs to manage menus, orders, and receive payments efficiently.
Delivery Driver: Needs to receive delivery assignments, navigate efficiently, and update delivery status.
functional_requirements: User registration and authentication (customer, restaurant, driver).
Browse restaurants and their menus with search and filter options.
Add/remove items from a shopping cart.
Secure payment processing integration (credit card, digital wallets).
View order history and status updates.
Real-time GPS-based delivery tracking for customers.
Restaurant dashboard for menu management, order acceptance, and order status updates.
Driver application for receiving orders, route optimization, and updating delivery status.
Rating and review system for restaurants and drivers.
non_functional_requirements: **Performance**: The system must be fast and responsive, handling high concurrent user loads.
**Security**: All user data, payment information, and transactions must be securely protected and compliant with relevant regulations.
**Usability**: The user interface should be intuitive, easy to navigate, and accessible across different devices.
**Reliability**: The system should maintain high availability (e.g., 99.9% uptime) with robust error handling.
**Scalability**: The architecture must support future growth in users, restaurants, and order volume.
**Maintainability**: The codebase should be well-documented, modular, and easy to update and extend.
constraints: Must comply with data privacy regulations (e.g., GDPR, CCPA).
Integration with at least two major third-party payment gateways.
Development must adhere to a strict budget and timeline.
Dependence on reliable third-party mapping and GPS services for real-time tracking.
Compatibility with major mobile operating systems (iOS and Android).
assumptions: Users have stable internet access and compatible mobile devices.
Restaurants are willing to onboard and actively manage their presence on the platform.
A sufficient pool of delivery drivers is available and willing to use the driver application.
Third-party payment gateways and mapping services maintain their APIs and service levels.
Legal and regulatory environment regarding food delivery and gig economy remains stable.
out_of_scope: Loyalty programs or advanced reward systems for customers.
In-app customer support chat (initial focus on FAQ and external support).
Advanced business intelligence dashboards for restaurants (beyond basic reporting).
Subscription models for premium features for users or restaurants.
Internationalization/Multi-language support (initially targeting a single language and region).
Integration with point-of-sale (POS) systems within restaurants.
